description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white and light gray coloration, featuring subtle hints of brown and beige. The surface is rough and uneven, with jagged edges and a natural, weathered appearance, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. There are areas of glossy sheen, possibly due to moisture or mineral deposits, giving the rock a slightly reflective quality in certain spots. The rock's mass and density are apparent from its solid, compact form, and it appears to be composed mainly of stone, likely granite or quartzite, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and shape make it suitable as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park or landscaped area. No visible markings, graffiti, or signs of human modification are present on its surface.
description: A rough, irregular white and gray rock with jagged edges and a slightly glossy, weathered surface. It appears dense and solid, suitable as a natural barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view shows the rock's most prominent jagged edge and a mix of white and gray tones with some glossy patches.
description_view_1: Here, the rock appears flatter with more visible glossy areas and a smoother, less angular profile.
description_view_2: This angle highlights the broadest, flattest surface of the rock, dominated by white coloration and subtle texture.
description_view_3: The rock looks rounder and more uniform from this view, with a mostly white surface and minimal visible texture.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
